title: Design a Simple REST API for a Blog
tagline: Define basic CRUD endpoints for a standard blog application.
scenario: A client wants a basic headless CMS for their new tech blog. The system needs to support managing `Posts` and user `Comments` on those posts.
brief: Write a minimal REST API specification for managing Posts and Comments. Focus heavily on using the correct HTTP verbs (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) and standard URL routing conventions.
deliverables: A list of the necessary endpoints including the HTTP method and route path (e.g., `GET /posts`)
A brief description of what each endpoint does
A JSON payload example of what a successful `POST /posts` request body would look like
submissionGuidance: We are looking for standard REST conventions. Pay attention to how you nest routes (e.g., how do you fetch comments for a specific post?).

rubric.label: REST Routing
rubric.description: Are the route paths semantic and correctly pluralized?

rubric.label: HTTP Verbs
rubric.description: Are the correct HTTP verbs used for CRUD operations?

rubric.label: JSON Payload
rubric.description: Is the POST payload example realistic and properly formatted?
